authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 163, Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Homeland Security, U.S. Customs and Border Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary customs recordkeeping rule for brokers and importers, making it authoritative for what evidence must exist before a trade report can claim transaction or filing truth.

authoritative_sources.title: 15 CFR Part 30, Foreign Trade Regulations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Commerce, U.S. Census Bureau
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary export-reporting regulation governing AES filing, correction, filing responsibilities, and data elements, which defines the reporting workflow for U.S. global trade submissions.

authoritative_sources.title: ACE Reports: Enhancement of Automated Export System Universe to Replace Trade Export Data Universe
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CBP reporting notice describing the report universe and report-access workflow used by trade operators preparing operational reporting outputs.

authoritative_sources.title: AES Compliance Report
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Census compliance reference describing filing-quality defects, corrections, and error categories relevant to control verification and repeat-incident handling.

authoritative_sources.title: OFAC Sanctions List Service
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary sanctions-screening source relevant when counterparties, geographies, or vessels in a trade report may require hold or escalation rather than routine reporting treatment.

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory to Financial Institutions on Filing Suspicious Activity Reports regarding Trade-Based Money Laundering
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal AML advisory describing trade-based money laundering indicators and escalation expectations when trade-report anomalies may reflect suspicious activity.
used_for: scenario design for suspicious reporting patterns

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML InfoBase and Appendix L: SAR Quality Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized supervisory framework for suspicious activity monitoring, SAR quality, escalation governance, and record-retention expectations in financial-services-adjacent workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO 20022 Financial Messaging Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized standard for payment and reconciliation message semantics, useful for tying trade reports to settlement, remittance, and ledger records without field ambiguity.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and record-of-truth verification
workflow_stages.description: Collect the entry, export, broker, shipment, account, and transaction context and identify the authoritative record set before any reporting or disclosure output is prepared.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Report assembly and submission logic
workflow_stages.description: Determine report grain, source tie-outs, required fields, correction windows, and submission sequencing for the requested reporting package.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Control verification and discrepancy handling
workflow_stages.description: Apply quality controls to incomplete inputs, contradictory records, repeat defects, reconciliation mismatches, and hold or release decisions before clearing a package.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure and release decisioning
workflow_stages.description: Prepare quality or safety status, hold or release recommendations, and disclosure-risk notes while preserving caveats and authority limits.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, sanctions, and policy-interpretation escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route suspicious activity, sanctions sensitivity, customer-impact risk, and unresolved policy conflicts to the correct adjacent owner rather than resolving them inside the lane.

artifacts: ACE and AES reports, filings, and report-access references
Entry, entry-summary, amendment, and post-summary correction records
Schedule B or HTS-aligned commodity records and shipment references
Invoices, bills of lading, commercial documents, and valuation support
Reconciliation, remittance, settlement, and general-ledger extracts
KYC, AML, sanctions, fraud, dispute, servicing, and approval-history artifacts
Disclosures, notices, communication logs, and corrective-action records
systems_of_record: ACE, ABI, and AES reporting systems
ERP, finance, remittance, and general-ledger platforms

allowed: Prepare evidence-backed reporting packages, tie-out summaries, quality or safety status notes, and submission logic packages for in-scope trade reporting work.
Determine whether evidence supports hold, release recommendation, submission deferral, investigation, or corrective-action escalation.
Record assumptions, caveats, and source conflicts without implying unsupported legal or regulatory authority.
escalate: Required tenant facts, jurisdiction, source precedence, or retrieval dependencies materially change the answer.
Fraud, AML, sanctions, customer-impact, or legal-interpretation issues change the safe reporting posture.
Different systems produce unreconciled outcomes for the same reporting period, disclosure, or filing.
refuse: Requests for legal interpretation, policy override, sanctions override, or final regulator-facing approval.
Requests to clear a hold, certify a package, or submit while required evidence is missing or contradictory.
Requests to suppress suspicious activity, consumer-impact, or disclosure conflicts that need formal escalation.

domain_failure_modes: Treating incomplete or contradictory AES, ACE, broker, and finance extracts as if they establish one clean reporting truth.
Submitting or clearing a reporting package while a documented hold, correction window, or unresolved nonconformance still exists.
Normalizing possible TBML, sanctions, or ownership anomalies as ordinary reporting variances instead of escalating them.
Collapsing reconciliation conflicts across operational and financial systems without a disclosure-risk or corrective-action note.
Crossing from operational reporting support into legal interpretation, regulator-facing representation, or final approval authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Submission inputs are incomplete or conflicting
A requested claim is not fully supported by the evidence set

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Non-U.S. jurisdiction reporting calendars and post-filing correction rules
unresolved_ambiguities.reason: The public baseline is intentionally U.S.-anchored to CBP, Census, OFAC, and FinCEN sources. Tenant-supplied jurisdiction context is required at runtime before extending beyond that baseline.
